purpose: Define the per-user engine, queue, kill-switch, risk-limit, and order-intent safety contract required before external members can use live-readiness features.
non_goals: No app/engine/order_flow.py, app/risk/**, KIS broker, production DB, deployment, or live execution behavior is changed by this contract.
No order path, risk gate, kill switch, auto-trading flag, or KIS credential is enabled for external users.
No schema migration or Supabase project mutation is applied.
No secret, provider token, bank/payment API, or real customer record is read or written.

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: global system_state flags such as auto_trading_enabled, kill_switch_active, global_mode, and consecutive_order_failures
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select own engine state where auth.uid() = user_id; request mode changes only through backend validation
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: engine worker receives an explicit user_id context and reads only that user's state

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: single process run_once scans all active conditions and uses a process lock plus condition CAS
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: member can request own queued dry run only after profile and safety preconditions
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: queue claim key includes user_id and queue item id; one user's run cannot claim another user's item

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: trade_conditions table lacks user_id and is processed globally
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select/write own conditions where auth.uid() = user_id through backend validation
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: engine loads only active conditions for the current user_id context

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: kill switch, auto trading, autonomy mode, market halt, VI, and disclosure flags live in global system_state keys
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select own flags and request permitted own flags through backend validation
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: SafetyChecker reads user-scoped flags first and has no cross-user fallback for live execution

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: risk_limits has a GLOBAL scope and SafetyChecker reads get_global_risk_limit()
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select own risk limits and request constrained own limit changes through backend validation
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: SafetyChecker uses the current user's risk limit for every order intent

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: consecutive_order_failures and daily realized PnL checks are global state/log derived
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: failure counters and daily loss calculations are per user and disable only that user's auto trading

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: order request is built directly from a condition inside OrderFlow
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: append own intent only through backend risk gate; select own intent/audit status
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: broker order can be placed only from a validated user-owned append-only intent
runtime_surfaces.admin_policy: server-audited support read only; no silent mutation

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: order_logs reference condition_id but lack user_id
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select own logs where auth.uid() = user_id
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: every order log includes user_id and order_intent_id before any broker call

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: execution_logs join order_logs and inherit global owner-oriented scope
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: select own executions where auth.uid() = user_id
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: every execution log inherits user_id from its order log and broker account context

runtime_surfaces.current_local_state: Notifier messages can be process/global and include condition/order summaries
runtime_surfaces.member_policy: receive only own notification destination and own event summary
runtime_surfaces.worker_policy: notifier destination is derived from the user-owned integration metadata for the same user_id

safety_invariants.statement: Every production engine state, queue, condition, risk, order intent, order log, execution log, circuit breaker, and notification row must include user_id.
External members cannot share a single global auto_trading_enabled flag; auto trading is per user and fail-closed by default.
Kill switch state is per user, and an Owner global emergency stop may only add a broader stop, never bypass a user's stop.
Max order amount, daily amount, circuit-breaker threshold, and one-share exception are user-scoped before external members can trade.
Engine worker queue claims include user_id so one user's run cannot claim or mutate another user's condition.
Production order intents are append-only records created after backend validation and before any broker order call.
OrderFlow and SafetyChecker receive a user_id or equivalent tenant context before processing any production order intent.
KIS app key, app secret, access token, account reference, and broker client context are scoped to the same user_id as the order intent.
SafetyChecker reads user-scoped flags, limits, holdings/log aggregates, and market blocks before allowing a production order.
Consecutive failure and daily loss circuit breakers disable only the affected user's auto trading, unless an audited global emergency stop is active.
Owner/admin overrides of engine, risk, queue, or order-intent state are server-side and audited with actor_user_id and target_user_id.
This contract does not authorize live execution; live/member execution remains blocked until production schema, RLS, KIS terms, and Owner approval exist.

forbidden_fallbacks: global auto flag for member execution
global risk limit for member execution
global KIS credential for member execution
client-side tenant filter as security boundary
failure_behavior: missing user-owned KIS credential blocks execution
missing per-user risk limit blocks execution
missing append-only order intent blocks execution
any cross-user mismatch blocks execution and creates audit evidence
